title: Testing Project Daily Thread
reminderBody: The task prompt should clearly describe the expected behavior so contributors do not need to guess hidden requirements.
goodExample: Update the checkout form so the submit button remains disabled until all required fields contain valid values.
badExample: Improve the checkout form and make it work better.
quickRule: If it will be evaluated, it should be clearly stated.

title: Testing Project Alignment Day
reminderBody: Tests should verify the result experienced by the user rather than checking for specific function names, files, or code patterns.
goodExample: The test submits the form and verifies that a success message appears.
badExample: The test checks whether the source file contains the string submitForm.
quickRule: A test should fail when the behavior is broken, not when the implementation looks different.

title: Testing Project Quality Reminder
reminderBody: Each rubric criterion should evaluate one clear and independently measurable requirement.
goodExample: The cart total updates after the user removes an item.
badExample: The cart works correctly, looks good, updates totals, and supports checkout.
quickRule: If one part can pass while another fails, split the criterion.
